result.stdout:
Description:
  DotNet.Pdf v0.4 [merge, split, convert, extract PDF documents and pages]

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  split                Split PDF
  merge                Merge PDFs
  convert              Convert PDF to images
  imagetopdf           Convert image to PDF
  text                 Extract PDF Text
  bookmarks            Extract PDF Bookmarks (outlines)
  info                 Extract PDF Information
  rotate               Rotate PDF pages
  remove               Remove pages from PDF
  insert               Insert blank pages into PDF
  reorder              Reorder PDF pages
  list-attachments     List PDF attachments
  extract-attachments  Extract PDF attachments
  list-objects         List all objects on a page
  list-forms           List all form fields in a document
  watermark            Add a text or image watermark

commands.payload:
Description:
  DotNet.Pdf v0.4 [merge, split, convert, extract PDF documents and pages]

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  split                Split PDF
  merge                Merge PDFs
  convert              Convert PDF to images
  imagetopdf           Convert image to PDF
  text                 Extract PDF Text
  bookmarks            Extract PDF Bookmarks (outlines)
  info                 Extract PDF Information
  rotate               Rotate PDF pages
  remove               Remove pages from PDF
  insert               Insert blank pages into PDF
  reorder              Reorder PDF pages
  list-attachments     List PDF attachments
  extract-attachments  Extract PDF attachments
  list-objects         List all objects on a page
  list-forms           List all form fields in a document
  watermark            Add a text or image watermark

result.stdout:
Description:
  Extract PDF Bookmarks (outlines)

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf bookmarks [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --password <password>  pdf password
  --format <format>      output format [text json xml] [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf bookmarks [options]

  --format <format>      output format [text json xml] [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert PDF to images

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf convert [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --output <output>      Output Directory
  --range <range>        page range
  --password <password>  pdf password
  --encoder <encoder>    encoder [.png, .jpg, .gif, .bmp] [default: .jpg]
  --dpi <dpi>            output dpi [default: 200]
  --names <names>        output filenames, ex: {original}-{page}
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Convert PDF to images

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf convert [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --output <output>      Output Directory
  --range <range>        page range
  --password <password>  pdf password
  --encoder <encoder>    encoder [.png, .jpg, .gif, .bmp] [default: .jpg]
  --dpi <dpi>            output dpi [default: 200]
  --names <names>        output filenames, ex: {original}-{page}
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Extract PDF attachments

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf extract-attachments [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --output <output>      output directory for extracted attachments
  --password <password>  pdf password
  --index <index>        extract specific attachment by index (0-based). If not specified, all attachments are extracted
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Insert blank pages into PDF

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf insert [options]

Options:
  --input <input>          input pdf filename
  --output <output>        output pdf filename
  --positions <positions>  insert positions in format 'position:count' (e.g., 1:2,5:1 to insert 2 pages at position 1 and 1 page at position 5)
  --password <password>    pdf password
  --width <width>          width of blank pages in points [default: 612]
  --height <height>        height of blank pages in points [default: 792]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --password <password>    pdf password
  --width <width>          width of blank pages in points [default: 612]
  --height <height>        height of blank pages in points [default: 792]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Merge PDFs

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf merge [options]

Options:
  --input <input>                      multiple filenames to merge
  --output <output>                    output pdf filename
  --input-script <input-script>        text file with list of files to merge, separated by newlines
  --input-directory <input-directory>  merge all files inside this directory
  --recursive                          Look for PDFs recursively [default: False]
  --password <password>                pdf password for input files
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Merge PDFs

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf merge [options]

Options:
  --input <input>                      multiple filenames to merge
  --output <output>                    output pdf filename
  --input-script <input-script>        text file with list of files to merge, separated by newlines
  --input-directory <input-directory>  merge all files inside this directory
  --recursive                          Look for PDFs recursively [default: False]
  --password <password>                pdf password for input files
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Remove pages from PDF

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf remove [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --output <output>      output pdf filename
  --pages <pages>        comma-separated list of page numbers to remove (e.g., 1,3,5)
  --password <password>  pdf password
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Reorder PDF pages

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf reorder [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --output <output>      output pdf filename
  --order <order>        comma-separated list of page numbers in new order (e.g., 3,1,2,4)
  --password <password>  pdf password
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Rotate PDF pages

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf rotate [options]

Options:
  --input <input>        input pdf filename
  --output <output>      output pdf filename
  --range <range>        page range to rotate
  --password <password>  pdf password
  --rotation <rotation>  rotation angle (90, 180, 270 degrees) [default: 90]
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Split PDF

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf split [options]

Options:
  --input <input>                  input pdf filename
  --output <output>                Output Directory
  --range <range>                  page range
  --password <password>            pdf password
  --use-bookmarks                  use bookmarks for names [default: False]
  --names <names>                  output filenames, ex: {original}-{page}
  --output-script <output-script>  text file with list of files to split to, separated by newlines
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add a text or image watermark

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf watermark [options]

Options:
  --input <input> (REQUIRED)    Input PDF file
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)  Output PDF file
  --password <password>         PDF password
  --text <text>                 Watermark text
  --image <image>               Watermark image path
  --font <font>                 Font name for text watermark [default: Helvetica]
  --font-size <font-size>       Font size for text watermark [default: 50]
  --color <color>               Color for text watermark (R,G,B) [default: 128,128,128]
  --opacity <opacity>           Opacity from 0 (transparent) to 255 (opaque) [default: 50]
  --rotation <rotation>         Rotation angle in degrees [default: 45]
  --scale <scale>               Scale factor for image watermark [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Add a text or image watermark

Usage:
  dotnet.pdf watermark [options]

Options:
  --input <input> (REQUIRED)    Input PDF file

  --password <password>         PDF password
  --text <text>                 Watermark text
  --image <image>               Watermark image path

  --opacity <opacity>           Opacity from 0 (transparent) to 255 (opaque) [default: 50]
  --rotation <rotation>         Rotation angle in degrees [default: 45]
  --scale <scale>               Scale factor for image watermark [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information
